channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Senator Jeff Flake remembers his colleague and friend Lindsay Graham
item.description: Former Republican Senator Jeff Flake served in congress alongside Lindsay Graham from 2013-2019. Despite moments of tension and disagreement, he says he and Sen. Graham were "friends to the end."
item.content:encoded: Former Republican Senator Jeff Flake served in congress alongside Lindsay Graham from 2013-2019. Despite moments of tension and disagreement, he says he and Sen. Graham were "friends to the end."
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: What would it mean for Ukraine if it actually starts producing Patriot missiles?
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Seth Jones from the Center for Strategic and International Studies about President Trump granting Ukraine permission to produce Patriot missiles.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Why do baseball movies make for some of the most enduring sports movies?
item.description: Baseball isn't just America's pastime, it's also the backdrop for many of our greatest sports movies.
item.content:encoded: Baseball isn't just America's pastime, it's also the backdrop for many of our greatest sports movies.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Georgia's unique in the American Revolution told through its historical markers
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Andrew Iden, Executive Producer of Marked!: The Podcast, which examines Georgia's role in America's revolutionary period through the 2,000 roadside historical marker.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Andrew Iden, Executive Producer of Marked!: The Podcast, which examines Georgia's role in America's revolutionary period through the 2,000 roadside historical marker.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Got a long summer reading list? NPR's Life Kit helps you keep pages turning
item.description: Many people dream of extended summer reading time, but to really dig into books, you need steal any moment possible.
item.content:encoded: Many people dream of extended summer reading time, but to really dig into books, you need steal any moment possible.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: A newly issued Oscar Peterson jazz album takes us back in time to a Detroit nightclub
item.description: Long lost tapes of an Oscar Peterson piano performance in Detroit take us back in time to the night the jazz session was recorded.
item.content:encoded: Long lost tapes of an Oscar Peterson piano performance in Detroit take us back in time to the night the jazz session was recorded.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71
item.description: His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.
item.content:encoded:
His office said Graham died Saturday night after a "brief and sudden illness." The Republican senator was instrumental in enacting Trump's policy and staffing priorities.

(Image credit: Jeffrey Collins)
item.dc:creator: Eric McDaniel

item.title: Palestinian aid worker who organized World Cup screenings killed in Israeli strike
item.description: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.content:encoded: Palestinians are mourning Mohammed al-Wahidi, a beloved aid worker in Gaza. He was killed by as Israeli airstrike while en route to a World Cup screening which he organized.
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: New security system causes major delays at Europe's airports amid peak travel season
item.description: The European Union recently implemented a new security system to better monitor foreigners who enter and exit. But its messy rollout has upended the summer travel season.
item.content:encoded: The European Union recently implemented a new security system to better monitor foreigners who enter and exit. But its messy rollout has upended the summer travel season.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: President Trump says ceasefire with Iran is over. What happens next?
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Ariane Tabatabai, Vice President of Research, Security and Defense, at the Chicago Council on Global Affairs, about developments in the war between the U.S. and Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea speaks with Ariane Tabatabai, Vice President of Research, Security and Defense, at the Chicago Council on Global Affairs, about developments in the war between the U.S. and Iran.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea
